Consejos-Matias-Pocho-Poisson.title: Consejos-Matias-Pocho-Poisson
Consejos-Matias-Pocho-Poisson.content:
Consejos de Matías Pocho Poisson para viaje a Disney/Universal

Recopilación de recomendaciones reales de un viajero experimentado. Fuente: chat personal con Rodrigo.

🏨 Alojamiento y transporte

Si te alojás en hoteles Disney → usar el transporte interno de Disney para moverte a los parques; funciona bien y te evitás el auto.
Si no estás en hotel Disney → alquilar auto es útil sobre todo para compras, no tanto para parques.
Contexto general → Orlando es muy comercial: “te cobran hasta por respirar”, así que conviene planificar gastos.

🛍️ Compras (shopping)

Evitar malls tradicionales de Orlando → “no tienen nada”.
Recomendados:

Vineland Premium Outlets → mejor variedad y precios.
International Mall → muy promocionado y rendidor.
Ross Dress for Less → barato, ir temprano.


Aclaraciones:

Los outlets suelen ser al aire libre → si llueve es un garrón.
A veces están explotados de gente y con poco stock.


Consejo clave → llevar un carry-on extra por persona solo para compras.

🎢 Parques Disney – estrategia general

Ritmo → No hacer todos los parques seguidos porque terminás destruido; conviene intercalar días de descanso.
Llegada cansada → Si llegás cansado del viaje, reacomodar el plan en Disney Springs: ahí te atienden y te resuelven todo muy bien.
Día libre → Disney Springs es lindo para pasear un día libre.
Clima → En la época que vas (abril), ya hace calor (≈30°).

📱 App y planificación

App Disney → Instalar sí o sí la app de Disney: desde ahí gestionás todo y usás el mapa interactivo para no caminar al pedo.
Lightning Pass:

Se compran antes, no el mismo día, y tenés que programar el día de uso.
El Lightning Pass por parque incluye casi todos los juegos, menos los top.


Cola virtual → Algunos juegos tienen cola virtual (ej. Guardianes de la Galaxia) y la app te avisa cuándo ir.

⚡ Juegos para los que recomienda pase rápido SÍ o SÍ

Tron (Magic Kingdom)
Guardianes de la Galaxia (Epcot)
Star Wars (Hollywood Studios)
Avatar (Animal Kingdom)


Para él, el mejor juego es Guardianes, después Avatar, Star Wars y Tron.

🎢 Opiniones sobre juegos (útiles para decidir)

Tron → muy rápida, posición incómoda; si no te gustan las montañas rusas, “llevá pañales”.
Avatar → simulador muy realista, de lo mejor.
Simpsons (Universal) → divertido pero puede dar motion sickness.
Rápido y Furioso → juego flojo, pero suele tener poca fila.
Jimmy Fallon (Universal) → sorprendentemente bueno.

🎒 Mochilas, lockers y comida

Mochilas → Nada peor que ir cargado con mochilas todo el día.
Lockers → Conviene alquilar lockers (≈10 USD el día).
En Disney:

Podés llevar comida y bebida sin problema.
Llevar snacks, vianda, protector solar, etc.
Comprar bebidas en Walmart antes → adentro te matan con los precios.


En Universal:

Algunos juegos no permiten subir con cosas, pero varios tienen lockers gratis del propio juego.



🍔 Comida en los parques

General → La comida es cara y mediocre: hamburguesas malas y precios absurdos.
Recomendación → No conviene comer dentro de los parques salvo necesidad.


Para compartir con Mariangeles

Este archivo está listo para enviar a Mariangeles o consultar en la planificación. Contiene consejos prácticos de un viajero real, no de guías genéricas.

Puntos clave para conversar con ella:

Mochilas vs lockers → Evaluar si conviene alquilar locker diario (≈10 USD) para no cargar peso.
Compras → Llevar un carry-on extra solo para compras.
Ritmo → Intercalar días de descanso para no terminar destruidos.
Juegos top → Tron, Guardianes, Star Wars, Avatar → priorizar pase rápido.
Comida → Llevar snacks y bebidas desde afuera (Walmart) para no pagar precios altos adentro.

Disney/Overview.title: Overview
Disney/Overview.content:
Disney Overview
Trip-specific context

Stay: Disney’s Pop Century Resort
Ticket type: 4-day, 1 park per day
Park dates: 18–24 Apr 2026 (6-night stay; 4 park days + flexibility)
Planning implication: every Disney park day matters because there is no hopper flexibility built in.


Disney park set

Magic Kingdom
EPCOT
Disney’s Hollywood Studios
Disney’s Animal Kingdom

Strategy pages

Early Theme Park Entry — Pop Century early entry perks
Lightning Lane — LL Multi Pass, Single Pass, per-park tier lists
April 2026 Context — closures, crowd patterns, seasonal context
Dining Reservations — booking window, hard ADRs, urgency note


Park day sequencing — recommendation
You have 4 parks and 6 nights. This leaves 2 days for resort, Disney Springs, rest, or re-visits.
Suggested order based on energy and crowd logic






























Day suggestionParkReasoningDay 1 DisneyDisney’s Hollywood StudiosBest park to rope-drop hard at the start of the Disney block while energy is fresh. Headliners load fast. No major nighttime anchor required if tired.Day 2 DisneyDisney’s Animal KingdomEarly morning advantage for safari + Pandora. Easiest park to finish by afternoon — good for mid-trip recovery.Day 3 DisneyMagic KingdomFull day justified; castle + fireworks payoff. Can absorb a more relaxed pace if earlier days were tiring.Day 4 DisneyEPCOTFlexible start; Flower & Garden Festival adds food/atmosphere payoff. Best park for a later arrival if fatigued. Strong evening close with Luminous.
Alternative: If you want the fireworks experience earlier in the trip (e.g., on day 1 or 2), swap Magic Kingdom earlier. The above order is for efficiency; the right order depends on preferences.

High-level sequencing logic

Hollywood Studios and Animal Kingdom reward strong early starts most.
Magic Kingdom justifies a full day because it combines volume + nighttime payoff (fireworks).
EPCOT is the most flexible — can start later and still have a full, valuable day due to the Flower & Garden Festival.


Critical April 2026 planning notes

Big Thunder Mountain Railroad is CLOSED through early May 2026 — remove from all MK plans.
Rock ‘n’ Roller Coaster is CLOSED for Muppets retheme — remove from all DHS plans.
EPCOT Flower & Garden Festival runs your entire Disney stay — plan it into the EPCOT day.
Dining reservation window is open now — if ADRs not booked, act immediately. See Dining Reservations.
Lightning Lane Single Passes for TRON, Rise of the Resistance, Guardians, and Flight of Passage should be booked 7 days before each park day (Pop Century resort benefit).


Open planning question
Which Disney park is least important emotionally? That answer shapes where to place a lighter day or absorb fatigue — likely EPCOT given it works well as a later-start, food-forward day.

Disney/Parks/Disney's-Animal-Kingdom.title: Disney's Animal Kingdom
Disney/Parks/Disney's-Animal-Kingdom.content:
Disney’s Animal Kingdom

Park role
Best Disney park for a sharp morning plan. Strong visual atmosphere, excellent headliners, and often easier to finish earlier than the other Disney parks.
Best use case on this trip
Ideal if you want one Disney day that is efficient, scenic, and less dependent on staying deep into the night.
Current trip fit

Strong current candidate for Thu 23 Apr 2026.
This placement works well if 22 Apr stays as a shopping / flex day and if you want the final Disney park day to be efficient rather than late-night heavy.
Also fits well as a park where a sharp morning matters more than a long closing-time commitment.

Early-entry posture
See Early Theme Park Entry. This is one of the strongest parks for using early entry aggressively — primarily for Pandora.
Lightning Lane posture
See Lightning Lane for full tier lists and strategy. Animal Kingdom has no tiers — all Multi Pass rides are in one pool.

Single Pass (buy separately): Avatar: Flight of Passage — book at 7 AM; frequently sells out by mid-morning on busy days
Multi Pass (any 3): Expedition Everest, Kilimanjaro Safaris, Na’vi River Journey, Kali River Rapids, Festival of the Lion King, Finding Nemo: The Big Blue… and Beyond!, Feathered Friends in Flight, Zootopia: Better Zoogether!

Recommended LLMP pick: Expedition Everest + Kilimanjaro Safaris + Na’vi River Journey.
Recommended LLSP: Flight of Passage.

Land layout and attraction clustering



































LandKey attractionsNotesOasis / Discovery IslandTree of Life, It’s Tough to be a Bug!Entry hub; Tree of Life walkthrough trails herePandora – The World of AvatarAvatar: Flight of Passage, Na’vi River JourneyFar left of park; built around blue bioluminescence at night. Best if visited at opening or at nightAfricaKilimanjaro Safaris, Gorilla Falls Exploration Trail, Festival of the Lion KingSafari is Africa; Festival of the Lion King theater is also hereAsiaExpedition Everest, Kali River Rapids, Maharajah Jungle Trek, Finding Nemo: The Big Blue…and Beyond!Right side of park; Everest is the dominant coasterDinoLand U.S.A.Zootopia: Better Zoogether! (in renovation from DINOSAUR)Back right; DINOSAUR replaced by Zootopia attraction
Routing tip for a strong morning: Pandora first (Flight of Passage or Na’vi via LL) while standby is shortest. Then cross to Kilimanjaro Safaris. Then Expedition Everest. This covers three different quadrants — plan the sequence before entering the park.

Attraction list
Anchor attractions

Avatar: Flight of Passage (Single Pass — 44” height req; book LLSP at 7 AM)
Kilimanjaro Safaris (Multi Pass — no height req)
Expedition Everest (Multi Pass — 44” height req)
Festival of the Lion King (Multi Pass — show, no height req)
Na’vi River Journey (Multi Pass — no height req)

Strong secondary attractions

Maharajah Jungle Trek (no LL — self-paced animal walk-through)
Gorilla Falls Exploration Trail (no LL — self-paced animal walk-through)
Kali River Rapids (Multi Pass — 38” height req; expect to get wet)
Finding Nemo: The Big Blue… and Beyond! (Multi Pass — show)
Zootopia: Better Zoogether! (Multi Pass — verify operating details closer to trip)
Tree of Life Trails / Awakening (no LL — ambient/walkthrough)

No major nighttime anchor
Animal Kingdom closes earlier than other Disney parks. Pandora glows at night but late park hours are not guaranteed. Plan to be mostly done by late afternoon unless you verify extended evening hours.

Strategy sketch

Morning / early entry: Pandora — either Flight of Passage standby before it gets long, or Na’vi Journey if FoP has Single Pass.
Midday: Kilimanjaro Safaris (also strong in early morning when animals are most active). Expedition Everest via LLMP.
Afternoon: Trails, shows, lower-intensity fills. Kali River Rapids if weather is right.
This is the easiest Disney park to finish and leave by late afternoon, especially if shows and trails are not a priority.

Friction points

If you waste the morning, Flight of Passage becomes hard to recover efficiently.
Heat is amplified because many experiences are outdoors and mid-park.
The park can feel “done” early unless shows and trails matter to you.
Kilimanjaro Safaris is best in the morning for animal activity — midday heat puts animals in shade.

Open questions

Is Flight of Passage the clear must-do morning priority?
Do the animal trails and stage shows add real value, or is this mostly a headliner day?
Would you prefer to leave by mid-afternoon and pair with a lighter evening elsewhere?

See also

Ranked — full attraction list ranked by community score
Overview — dining options and public scores
Lightning Lane — full LL tier list and booking strategy
Early Theme Park Entry — early entry approach

Disney/Parks/Disney's-Animal-Kingdom/Att.filePath: Disney/Parks/Disney's Animal Kingdom/Attractions/Avatar Flight of Passage.md
Disney/Parks/Disney's-Animal-Kingdom/Att.title: Avatar Flight of Passage
Disney/Parks/Disney's-Animal-Kingdom/Att.content:
Avatar: Flight of Passage

Type

Simulator (bike-mount flying simulator with screen and physical motion)

Location

Land: Pandora – The World of Avatar, Disney’s Animal Kingdom
Far left of the park; the most visited area of Animal Kingdom

Community sentiment score

9.8/10 — Sentiment tier: Elite
Basis: public-source consensus reviewed Mar 2026. Planning guidance, not an official rating.

What it is

Guests sit astride a “mountain banshee” simulator bike that pitches, rolls, and responds physically while a large screen projects an immersive flight over Pandora. Scent, mist, and wind effects augment the experience. One of the most highly rated theme park rides in the world.
Ride duration: ~5 min (feels longer due to immersion)

Physical characteristics

Height requirement: 44” (112 cm)
Intensity: Moderate — the physical sensations are strong (pitching forward and leaning into banking turns) but no g-forces or drops. The bike seat presses against the rider’s chest, which some guests find claustrophobic.
Motion sickness risk: High for guests sensitive to simulated flight/virtual reality motion. The immersion is deep — more than most Disney simulators.
Water: Light mist effects only
Drop: No physical drop; simulated descent visuals

Lightning Lane

Single Pass only — not included in Multi Pass
Approx. price: 15–19/person (dynamic)
Book at 7 AM (7 days ahead via Pop Century resort benefit) — frequently sells out by 9 AM on busy days
This is the clearest must-buy Single Pass at Animal Kingdom

Strategy notes

LLSP is essential — Flight of Passage standby can hit 90–120+ min by mid-morning on moderate crowd days.
Morning timing: Even with LLSP, the Pandora land becomes very crowded early. Consider arriving at Animal Kingdom before park opening and walking toward Pandora immediately.
Na’vi River Journey is adjacent (also in Pandora) — good cluster. Na’vi is Multi Pass, easier to manage alongside FoP via LL refresh.
After Pandora: Cross to Africa for Kilimanjaro Safaris (best when animals are most active in the morning).

Trip relevance
High — the most-loved attraction in Animal Kingdom and one of the highest-rated rides at Walt Disney World overall. Essential for this park day.
Planning note
Verify operating status closer to trip.

Disney/Parks/Disney's-Animal-Kingdom/Att.title: Kilimanjaro Safaris
Disney/Parks/Disney's-Animal-Kingdom/Att.content:
Kilimanjaro Safaris

Type

Vehicle tour (large open-air truck through real animal habitats; no track — the route is driven by a cast member)

Location

Land: Africa, Disney’s Animal Kingdom
Immediately in Africa land, a short walk from the park entrance hub

Community sentiment score

9.4/10 — Sentiment tier: Elite
Basis: public-source consensus reviewed Mar 2026. Planning guidance, not an official rating.

What it is

Open-air safari truck tour through a large savanna and forest habitat featuring real free-roaming animals including elephants, giraffes, lions, zebras, hippos, rhinos, flamingos, and more. Animals are not guaranteed to be visible or active but sightings are frequent. Each ride is different. Duration: ~18 min.

Physical characteristics

Height requirement: None
Intensity: Very low — bumpy road surfaces (intentional) but no thrills; suitable for all ages
Motion sickness risk: Very low (open air; no enclosed or simulated elements)
Water: No
Drop: No

When animals are most active

Early morning — before the heat of the day. Animals are most visible and active in the first 1–2 hours after park opening.
Midday heat drives many animals into shade or off-road areas; ride quality is noticeably lower.
This is a key reason Animal Kingdom rewards an early start.

Lightning Lane

Multi Pass — no tiers at Animal Kingdom; pick any 3 from the pool
Standby can reach 45–75 min mid-morning; worth booking via LLMP or riding early
Best morning strategy: Reach Kilimanjaro Safaris standby within the first 20 min of park opening (before or after early entry Pandora — decide based on priorities)

Strategy notes

If you must choose between early Flight of Passage or early Safari: Flight of Passage queue grows faster and has a Single Pass option; Safaris are better used in the first 30–45 min standby window, or via LLMP.
Adjacency: Africa cluster includes Gorilla Falls Exploration Trail (no LL — self-paced, good midday filler) and Festival of the Lion King theater (Multi Pass, show).

Trip relevance
High — Kilimanjaro Safaris is one of the most unique experiences in all of Walt Disney World, not replicated at any other Disney park. High priority for Animal Kingdom day.
Planning note
Verify operating status closer to trip. Animal availability is uncontrollable — all sightings vary by day.

Disney/Parks/Disney's-Hollywood-Studios.title: Disney's Hollywood Studios
Disney/Parks/Disney's-Hollywood-Studios.content:
Disney’s Hollywood Studios

Park role
Most top-heavy Disney park for headliners. Strong candidate for the most aggressive Disney rope drop.
Best use case on this trip
Best for a high-intensity day built around Star Wars, thrill rides, and a few major attractions that build long waits fast.
Current trip fit

Strong current candidate for Tue 21 Apr 2026.
This is another clean Pop Century + Skyliner fit and probably one of the highest-value Disney mornings to execute aggressively.
If EPCOT stays on 20 Apr, this sequence creates a sensible two-day Skyliner block before the later flex/shopping candidate day.

Early-entry posture
See Early Theme Park Entry. This is one of the highest-value parks for executing early entry well — use it to get one headliner before standby compounds.
Lightning Lane posture
See Lightning Lane for full tier lists and strategy. Summary:

Single Pass (buy separately): Star Wars: Rise of the Resistance — don’t skip; standby can hit 80–120 min
Multi Pass Tier 1 (max 1 advance): Slinky Dog Dash, Mickey & Minnie’s Runaway Railway, Millennium Falcon: Smugglers Run
Multi Pass Tier 2: The Twilight Zone Tower of Terror, Toy Story Mania!, Star Tours, Indiana Jones Epic Stunt Spectacular, Alien Swirling Saucers, Frozen Sing-Along

Recommended LLMP pick: Tier 1 = Slinky Dog Dash or Runaway Railway; Tier 2 = Tower of Terror + Toy Story Mania!
Recommended LLSP: Rise of the Resistance.

April 2026 closures — important













AttractionStatusRock ‘n’ Roller CoasterUndergoing Muppets retheme — very likely closed during your Apr 18–24 stay
Rock ‘n’ Roller Coaster is expected to reopen as a Muppets-themed coaster in Summer 2026. It is almost certainly not available during your Disney stay. Remove it from planning versions.

Land layout and attraction clustering



































Land / AreaKey attractionsNotesHollywood Blvd & Echo LakeIndiana Jones Epic Stunt Spectacular, Star Tours, For the First Time in Forever (Frozen Sing-Along)Entry corridor area; Indy show is Echo Lake areaSunset BoulevardTower of Terror, Rock ‘n’ Roller Coaster (closed), Fantasmic! amphitheaterTower is the dominant ride here; Coaster closedToy Story LandSlinky Dog Dash, Toy Story Mania!, Alien Swirling SaucersCompact area, opens early; often very crowded by 10 AMStar Wars: Galaxy’s EdgeRise of the Resistance, Millennium Falcon: Smugglers RunFar back of the park; most immersive areaGrand Ave / Animation CourtyardMickey & Minnie’s Runaway RailwayCenter of park
Routing tip: The efficient morning loop depends on your LL strategy. If using Rise LLSP + a Tier 1 LLMP: rope drop Toy Story Land (Slinky Dog or Runaway Railway standby), then pivot to Galaxy’s Edge when Rise LL window opens. Tower of Terror midday or afternoon via LLMP.

Attraction list
Anchor attractions

Star Wars: Rise of the Resistance (Single Pass — no height req)
Millennium Falcon: Smugglers Run (Multi Pass Tier 1 — 38” height req)
Slinky Dog Dash (Multi Pass Tier 1 — 38” height req)
The Twilight Zone Tower of Terror (Multi Pass Tier 2 — 40” height req)
Mickey & Minnie’s Runaway Railway (Multi Pass Tier 1 — no height req)
Rock ‘n’ Roller Coaster (Closed; retheme in progress — 48” height req when open)

Strong secondary attractions

Toy Story Mania! (Multi Pass Tier 2)
Alien Swirling Saucers (Multi Pass Tier 2 — 32” height req)
Star Tours – The Adventures Continue (Multi Pass Tier 2 — 40” height req)
Indiana Jones Epic Stunt Spectacular (Multi Pass Tier 2 — show)
For the First Time in Forever: A Frozen Sing-Along Celebration (Multi Pass Tier 2 — show)

Nighttime / entertainment value

Fantasmic! — lagoon/pyro/projection nighttime show; worth planning around if it’s a priority
Galaxy’s Edge atmosphere at night — strong immersion value even if rides are done


Strategy sketch

Morning / early entry: Prioritize Toy Story Land (Slinky Dog standby is shortest at opening) or use early entry to reach Galaxy’s Edge before it crowds.
Midday: Use LL refreshes for Tower of Terror, Toy Story Mania!. Shows (Indy, Frozen Sing-Along) work well during heat peak.
Evening: Either Fantasmic! or extended Galaxy’s Edge time. Decide before the evening because Fantasmic! seating works better with early positioning.

Friction points

This park has less filler than Magic Kingdom — bad waits hurt more.
Headliners are concentrated and all build long queues fast.
Galaxy’s Edge is far from Toy Story Land, making same-morning loops expensive.
Fantasmic! timing forces an early evening decision.

Open questions

Is Star Wars the emotional center of this park day?

⚠️ Status: Closed — April 2026
This attraction is closed for a retheme. It is being refurbished as a Muppets-themed coaster. The expected reopening is Summer 2026 — it is very unlikely to be operating during your Apr 18–24 Disney stay. Remove it from all plan versions.

Disney/Parks/Disney's-Hollywood-Studios/.filePath: Disney/Parks/Disney's Hollywood Studios/Attractions/Star Wars - Rise of the Resistance.md
Disney/Parks/Disney's-Hollywood-Studios/.title: Star Wars - Rise of the Resistance
Disney/Parks/Disney's-Hollywood-Studios/.content:
Star Wars: Rise of the Resistance

Type

Dark ride / multi-system attraction (trackless dark ride + walk-through + simulator elements)

Location

Land: Star Wars: Galaxy’s Edge, Disney’s Hollywood Studios
Far back of the park, inside Galaxy’s Edge; requires walking through the immersive outdoor area to reach it

Community sentiment score

9.8/10 — Sentiment tier: Elite
Basis: public-source consensus reviewed Mar 2026. Planning guidance, not an official rating.

What it is

Massive multi-phase attraction combining a pre-show “capture” sequence, a detention cell walkthrough, and a final trackless dark ride through a full-scale Star Destroyer environment. One of the most technically complex theme park attractions in existence. Multiple separate rooms and vehicle sequences.
Total experience from pre-show to exit: ~18–22 min.

Physical characteristics

Height requirement: None (no height requirement — all ages can ride)
Intensity: Moderate — trackless vehicle movement with sudden direction changes, small drop sensation during one sequence, loud effects. Not a thrill coaster.
Motion sickness risk: Low-moderate — the scale of the effects and some vehicle movements can affect sensitive guests, but it is far less intense than a coaster or simulator
Water: No
Drop: One short drop during the hangar sequence (very brief, not steep)

Lightning Lane

Single Pass only — not included in Multi Pass
Approx. price: 20–25/person (highest-priced LLSP at WDW)
Frequently sells out on busy days; buy at 7 AM (or 7 days ahead via Pop Century resort benefit)
Do not skip the Single Pass — standby during peak hours can reach 80–120+ min

Strategy notes

Single Pass is the right call for almost every visit. The experience is worth the premium.
If doing standby: only viable in the first 30–45 min of the park opening or late in the evening.
Adjacency: Millennium Falcon: Smugglers Run is steps away in the same Galaxy’s Edge area (Multi Pass Tier 1). These two are the most natural cluster in the park but you cannot do both at rope drop on standby — LL one, standby the other, or separate them.
Galaxy’s Edge has strong nighttime atmosphere even after rides are completed — worth returning to in the evening for photos and ambiance.

Trip relevance
High — the highest-rated attraction at Hollywood Studios and one of the top rides at all of Walt Disney World.
Planning note
Verify operating status closer to trip; Rise of the Resistance has had periodic technical difficulties that cause unexpected closures.
Park: Disney’s Hollywood Studios

Disney/Parks/EPCOT.title: EPCOT
Disney/Parks/EPCOT.content:
EPCOT

Park role
Most flexible Disney park. Good blend of major rides, showcase strolling, food, and a strong nighttime finish.
Best use case on this trip
Works well as either:

a ride-first morning with a slower afternoon, or
a later-paced day built around food, countries, and the evening show.

Your visit (18–24 Apr 2026) falls during EPCOT International Flower & Garden Festival — this materially changes the EPCOT day. See Flower-Garden-Festival-2026 for booth list and strategy.
Current trip fit

Strong current candidate for Mon 20 Apr 2026.
This is one of the cleanest operational fits in the draft because Pop Century + Skyliner makes EPCOT easier to execute.
Good candidate for a park day that starts efficiently but does not need to feel as intense as Hollywood Studios.

Early-entry posture
See Early Theme Park Entry. Early entry is valuable here if the goal is to get ahead on one or two major rides before shifting to a slower World Showcase pace.
Lightning Lane posture
See Lightning Lane for full tier lists and strategy. Summary:

Single Pass (buy separately): Guardians of the Galaxy: Cosmic Rewind — do not skip this; standby can reach 60–90+ min
Multi Pass Tier 1 (max 1 advance): Frozen Ever After, Remy’s Ratatouille Adventure, Test Track (verify Test Track status — was under refurb late 2024)
Multi Pass Tier 2: Soarin’ Around the World, Mission: SPACE, Living with the Land, The Seas with Nemo & Friends, Spaceship Earth, Turtle Talk with Crush, Journey Into Imagination

Recommended LLMP pick: Tier 1 = Remy’s or Frozen; Tier 2 = Soarin’ + one more. Buy Guardians LLSP separately.

April 2026 seasonal context

Flower & Garden Festival runs 4 Mar – 1 Jun 2026 — fully active during your visit
20+ outdoor kitchen food booths; included with park admission
Festival booth hours: Tue–Thu 11:30 AM – 8:30 PM; Fri–Mon 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM
Garden Rocks Concert Series on select evenings (some need separate ticket — verify schedule)
Test Track: verify operational status — was under refurbishment in late 2024


EPCOT neighborhood layout
EPCOT is divided into four “neighborhoods” plus World Showcase. The park is large — routing matters.






























NeighborhoodKey attractionsNotesWorld Celebration (near Spaceship Earth)Spaceship Earth, Journey Into ImaginationPark entry area; festival booths hereWorld Discovery (right, back)Guardians of the Galaxy: Cosmic Rewind, Mission: SPACE, Test TrackFarthest from entrance; go here first if Guardians is a priorityWorld Nature (left, back)Soarin’ Around the World, Living with the Land, The Seas with Nemo & Friends, Turtle TalkCluster all of these — they are in The Land and The Seas pavilionsWorld Showcase (lagoon loop)All country pavilions, festival booths, table service restaurantsCan take 2–3 hours to fully loop; no need to do it all
Routing tip: Start at World Discovery (Guardians) or World Nature (Soarin’), not at Spaceship Earth. Spaceship Earth has no LL and lower standby than headliners. Festival booths open at 11:30 — complete rides before transitioning to World Showcase.

Attraction list
Anchor attractions

Guardians of the Galaxy: Cosmic Rewind (Single Pass — 42” height req)
Remy’s Ratatouille Adventure (Multi Pass Tier 1 — no height req)
Frozen Ever After (Multi Pass Tier 1 — no height req)
Soarin’ Around the World (Multi Pass Tier 2 — 40” height req)
Test Track (Multi Pass Tier 1 — verify status; 40” height req)
Spaceship Earth (Multi Pass Tier 2 — no height req)

Strong secondary attractions

Journey of Water, Inspired by Moana (no LL — walkthrough; low friction filler)
Mission: SPACE (Multi Pass Tier 2 — 44” height for Orange/intense; 40” for Green/mild)
Living with the Land (Multi Pass Tier 2 — no height req; interesting and usually short wait)
The Seas with Nemo & Friends (Multi Pass Tier 2)
Turtle Talk with Crush (Multi Pass Tier 2)
Gran Fiesta Tour Starring The Three Caballeros (no LL — low friction, good World Showcase warm-up)

Nighttime / entertainment value

Luminous – The Symphony of Us (lagoon nighttime show; no ticket beyond park admission)
Festival booths until 8:30–9:00 PM
World Showcase country pavilions in the evening have strong atmosphere


Strategy sketch

Morning / early entry: Hit Guardians LLSP + one Tier 1 ride (Remy’s or Frozen via LLMP).
Midday: Finish Soarin’ and other Tier 2 priorities. Festival booths open at 11:30 — start World Showcase loop.
Afternoon: Graze booths counterclockwise or clockwise; stop at table service if reserved.
Evening: Stay for Luminous at the lagoon; booths are open until 8:30–9:00 PM.

Friction points

This park is physically large. Unintentional backtracking burns time.
Festival booths create a “we’ll just snack around” pace trap if you’re not deliberate.
Guardians and Remy’s/Frozen all build waits fast — missing the early window means long standby or no ride.
World Showcase strolling is only rewarding if you allow time for it; rushing it wastes the festival context.

Open questions

Is EPCOT primarily a rides day, a food-festival day, or a hybrid?
How much do World Showcase country pavilions and cuisine matter?
Is Luminous worth building the evening around?
Is Guardians a must-do headliner or optional?

Disney/Parks/EPCOT/Attractions/Guardians.filePath: Disney/Parks/EPCOT/Attractions/Guardians of the Galaxy - Cosmic Rewind.md
Disney/Parks/EPCOT/Attractions/Guardians.title: Guardians of the Galaxy - Cosmic Rewind
Disney/Parks/EPCOT/Attractions/Guardians.content:
Guardians of the Galaxy: Cosmic Rewind

Type

Coaster (reverse-launch indoor coaster with rotating vehicles; “omnicoaster”)

Location

Neighborhood: World Discovery, EPCOT
Back-right of the park; inside a large pavilion (the “Wonders of Xandar” building)

Community sentiment score

9.8/10 — Sentiment tier: Elite
Basis: public-source consensus reviewed Mar 2026. Planning guidance, not an official rating.

What it is

Indoor roller coaster in a blacked-out building where vehicles rotate to face the screen and action at key moments. Launches backwards first, then forward. Soundtrack changes periodically (multiple songs in rotation — guests don’t always know which they’ll get). One of the longest indoor coasters at Walt Disney World.
Ride duration: ~3 min

Physical characteristics

Height requirement: 42” (107 cm)
Intensity: High — the backwards launch and vehicle rotation create strong spatial disorientation. Faster and more intense than most Disney attractions.
Motion sickness risk: High for guests sensitive to motion. The rotation combined with a dark enclosed space makes this one of the most likely Disney rides to affect sensitive guests.
Water: No
Drop: No significant drops; the intensity is from speed and rotation

Lightning Lane

Single Pass only — not included in Multi Pass
Approx. price: 16–22/person (dynamic)
Frequently sells out; book at 7 AM (7 days ahead as Pop Century guest)
Do not attempt standby on a busy day — can reach 60–90+ min within the first hour

Strategy notes

LLSP is the right call for almost all visits. The experience is worth the cost.
If you want to ride multiple songs/soundtracks: The rotating soundtrack is a major draw for fans; some guests ride twice. If this interests you, consider buying LLSP and also checking standby late in the evening.
Adjacency: Mission: SPACE and (when operating) Test Track are close in World Discovery. If Test Track is open: good cluster for one section of the morning.

Trip relevance
High — frequently ranked as the best or among the best rides at EPCOT. Essential for coaster fans.
Planning note
Verify operating status closer to trip.

Disney/Parks/EPCOT/Seasonal/Flower-Garde.title: Flower-Garden-Festival-2026
Disney/Parks/EPCOT/Seasonal/Flower-Garde.content:
EPCOT International Flower & Garden Festival 2026
Key facts

Dates: 4 Mar – 1 Jun 2026
Your EPCOT visit: 18–24 Apr 2026 (full overlap — the festival will be active)
Cost: Included with regular EPCOT admission; no extra ticket required
Booth hours: Tue–Thu 11:30 AM – 8:30 PM; Fri–Mon 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM


What it adds to an EPCOT day

Outdoor kitchen food booths throughout the park (~20+ booths, small-plate format ~5–10/item)
Character and floral topiaries throughout World Showcase — major photo opportunities
Garden Rocks Concert Series on select evenings (some evenings require a separate ticket)
Plant-based “Garden Graze” option if interested
New for 2026: Nectar hosted by AdventHealth (new outdoor kitchen); 75+ new menu items across all booths

Planning implication: This festival turns EPCOT into a food-stroll park, not just a ride park. Treat 2–3 hours of the afternoon/evening as dedicated booth time if that appeals.

Outdoor kitchen booths — 2026
Organized by EPCOT neighborhood. Booth locations do not require routing through specific lands.
World Celebration (near Spaceship Earth)

















BoothNotesFlorida FreshFlorida-grown produce and proteins; refreshing and lightEPCOT Farmers FeastOpen 4 Mar – 10 May only — may still be running at your visit; verify

World Discovery (near Guardians, Mission: SPACE)

























BoothNotesBRUNCHCOTBrunch-themed items; popularThe Citrus BlossomCitrus-forward drinks and small platesRefreshment Station (Coca-Cola)Coke product samples; light snacksConnections EateryIndoor QS; not a festival booth but available
World Showcase — around the lagoon




































































































BoothCountry areaNotesPineapple PromenadeNear front of World ShowcaseDole Whip + pineapple items; very popular, hits earlyThe Honey Bee-stroNear frontHoney-themed savory and sweet itemsTrowel & TrellisNear UKVegetable-forward, plant-based optionsNorthern BloomNear NorwayNordic-inspiredFleur de LysFrance pavilionDuck confit, pastries — usually strong; very popularLa Isla FrescaMexico pavilion areaFresh island-inspired; lighter fareTangierine Café: Flavors of the MedinaMoroccoShared with regular pavilion QSHanamiJapanJapanese-inspired small platesPrimavera KitchenItaly pavilion areaItalian-inspired; expect lines near ItalyBauernmarkt: Farmer’s MarketGermany pavilionBavarian/German items; pairs with SommerfestSommerfestGermanyBeer, pretzels, German itemsMagnolia TerraceAmerican Adventure areaSouthern US-inspiredLan YuanChina pavilionChinese-inspired small platesBlock & HansNear American AdventureCraft beer focusFunnel CakeNear American AdventureDeep-fried sweetsYorkshire County Fish and ChipsUK pavilionClassic fish & chips; perennial crowd-pleaserCanada Popcorn CartCanada pavilionFlavored popcornRefreshment OutpostBetween China and GermanyCoca-Cola Freestyle; quick drinks

Strategy for the festival day
If this is your rides + booths day

Start with park opening: Guardians LLSP + Remy’s or Frozen via LLMP while crowds are light.
After 2–3 major rides, pivot to World Showcase and start grazing booths when they open at 11:30 AM.
Route clockwise or counterclockwise around the lagoon — pick a direction and stick to it; doubling back costs 20+ minutes.
Plan 4–6 booth stops as a “meal” rather than sitting down at a table-service restaurant.
Fleur de Lys (France) and Hanami (Japan) are perennially well-reviewed; prioritize these.
End the evening with Luminous – The Symphony of Us at the lagoon if you’re staying late.

If this is your food / atmosphere day (later start acceptable)

Skip rope drop; arrive by 10 AM.
Aim for Guardians + Remy’s/Frozen LLSP/LLMP in the morning.
Full afternoon World Showcase booth stroll.
Finish with Luminous.

What to skip at the booths
No need to hit every booth. Refreshment Outpost and Refreshment Station are primarily drinks filler. Canada Popcorn Cart is a snack, not a meal stop. Block & Hans is good if craft beer is a priority; skip otherwise.

Garden Rocks Concert Series

Concerts typically happen on select evenings at the America Gardens Theatre (near American Adventure pavilion).
Some require a separate dining package or ticket; others are included with park admission but require a seat reservation.
Verify the April schedule on Disney’s official website closer to your visit.
If Luminous and a Garden Rocks concert fall on the same evening, prioritize based on preference — they overlap in timing.


Items to verify closer to trip

EPCOT Farmers Feast booth status (listed as 4 Mar – 10 May; may or may not be active at your late-April dates)
Garden Rocks Concert Series schedule and ticket requirements for April 18–24
Whether any new booths or menu updates have been added post-announcement

Disney/Parks/Magic-Kingdom.title: Magic Kingdom
Disney/Parks/Magic-Kingdom.content:
Magic Kingdom

Park role
Classic Disney payoff park. Highest nostalgia density and one of the easiest parks to justify as a full open-to-close day.
Best use case on this trip
Best when you want the most “Disney” feeling in one day: icons, classic rides, castle atmosphere, parade / fireworks payoff.
Current trip fit

Current candidate for Sat 18 Apr 2026 immediately after the Universal → Disney hotel transfer.
This is emotionally strong as a first Disney day, but it is also the Disney placement most worth pressure-testing because transfer-day friction could reduce its payoff.
If kept on 18 Apr, the plan should be realistic about check-out, check-in timing, and whether you want a full rope-drop attempt versus a later-start Disney day.

Early-entry posture
See Early Theme Park Entry. Default approach: arrive early and use the first wave on major priorities before standby lines compound.
Lightning Lane posture
See Lightning Lane for full tier lists and strategy. Summary:

Single Pass (buy separately): TRON Lightcycle / Run, Seven Dwarfs Mine Train
Multi Pass Tier 1 (max 1 advance): Space Mountain, Peter Pan’s Flight, Jungle Cruise, Tiana’s Bayou Adventure
Multi Pass Tier 2: Haunted Mansion, Pirates of the Caribbean, Buzz Lightyear, It’s a Small World, Monsters Inc., Mickey’s PhilharMagic, Dumbo, and others


April 2026 closures — important





















AttractionStatusBig Thunder Mountain RailroadClosed through early May 2026 — not available during your Disney stayRivers of America / Tom Sawyer Island / Liberty Square RiverboatPermanently closed Jul 2025 — being replaced by Cars/Piston Peak expansionBuzz Lightyear’s Space Ranger SpinReopening 8 Apr 2026 — available during your stay
Remove Big Thunder Mountain from any plan versions. It will not be operational 18–24 Apr.

Land layout and attraction clustering








































LandKey attractionsNotesTomorrowlandTRON Lightcycle / Run, Space Mountain, Buzz Lightyear, PeopleMover, Monsters Inc. Laugh FloorRight side of park; TRON is the farthest backFantasylandSeven Dwarfs Mine Train, Peter Pan’s Flight, Haunted Mansion*, It’s a Small World, Winnie the Pooh, Dumbo, PhilharMagicLargest land; behind the castleLiberty SquareHaunted Mansion, The Hall of PresidentsSmall area between Fantasyland and FrontierlandFrontierlandTiana’s Bayou Adventure (Big Thunder Mountain: CLOSED)Left side; under major construction for Cars expansionAdventurelandJungle Cruise, Pirates of the Caribbean, Magic CarpetsLeft side near entranceMain Street, U.S.A.Entry corridor, shops, no ridesDining and photos only
Routing tip: A clean loop is Main Street → Tomorrowland → Fantasyland → Liberty Square → (Frontierland) → Adventureland → back to hub. TRON and Seven Dwarfs are the two hardest to recover without LL — get one at rope drop, use Single Pass for the other.

Attraction list
Anchor attractions

 Run (Single Pass — 48” height req)
Seven Dwarfs Mine Train (Single Pass — 38” height req)
Haunted Mansion (Multi Pass Tier 2 — no height req)
Pirates of the Caribbean (Multi Pass Tier 2 — no height req)
Space Mountain (Multi Pass Tier 1 — 44” height req)
Peter Pan’s Flight (Multi Pass Tier 1 — no height req)
Tiana’s Bayou Adventure (Multi Pass Tier 1 — 40” height req)
Big Thunder Mountain Railroad (Closed through early May 2026)

Strong secondary attractions

Jungle Cruise (Multi Pass Tier 1)
Buzz Lightyear’s Space Ranger Spin (Multi Pass Tier 2; reopens 8 Apr 2026)
Tomorrowland Transit Authority PeopleMover (no LL — great filler)
It’s a Small World (Multi Pass Tier 2)
Carousel of Progress (no LL — good indoor break)
Mickey’s PhilharMagic (Multi Pass Tier 2)

Nighttime / entertainment value

Happily Ever After (fireworks show)
Festival of Fantasy Parade (daytime)
Castle atmosphere at night — worth staying for even after ride priorities are done


Strategy sketch

Morning / early entry: Hit TRON (standby is short in the first 15–30 min) or use early entry for Peter Pan + Haunted Mansion or Space Mountain while Single Pass users are still buying.
Midday: Shift to lower-friction classics, indoor shows, and LL refresh. Lunch break.
Afternoon: Finish remaining LLMP bookings. Explore Adventureland and Fantasyland.
Night: Reserve energy for Happily Ever After. Fireworks positioning can conflict with ride efficiency — decide before the evening.

Friction points

This park can absorb an entire day if you let it.
Priorities span the entire park; bad routing wastes 20–30 min per mistake.
Fireworks positioning (center hub or castle view) conflicts with being on the far sides of the park.
Construction in Frontierland area may affect routing and atmosphere.

Open questions

Is this your highest-emotion Disney day?
Are fireworks non-negotiable?
Do you want thrill focus, classic focus, or balanced first-time coverage?

Disney/Parks/Magic-Kingdom/Attractions/H.title: Haunted Mansion
Disney/Parks/Magic-Kingdom/Attractions/H.content:
Haunted Mansion

Type

Dark ride (slow-moving Doom Buggy vehicle; gothic humor theme)

Location

Land: Liberty Square, Magic Kingdom
Between Fantasyland and Frontierland; near Hall of Presidents

Community sentiment score

9.3/10 — Sentiment tier: Elite
Basis: public-source consensus reviewed Mar 2026. Planning guidance, not an official rating.

What it is

Classic Disney dark ride through a haunted estate with 999 “Happy Haunting Ghosts.” Stretching room entry sequence. Doom Buggy vehicles move through elaborately themed rooms. Mix of humor and mild spooky atmosphere. One of the most beloved and culturally significant rides at Disney World.
Ride duration: ~9 min (one of the longer Disney dark rides)

Physical characteristics

Height requirement: None
Intensity: Very low — slow, occasionally dark, a few mild jump-scare-style moments. Appropriate for essentially all ages.
Motion sickness risk: Very low
Water: No
Drop: There is a short descent (the “stretching room” drops slightly before the ride itself; it’s theatrical, not a thrill element)

Lightning Lane

Multi Pass Tier 2 — bookable as part of LLMP 3-ride selection
Standby can reach 40–60 min on busy days; on moderate late-April days may be manageable via standby during midday lull
Worth including as a Tier 2 LLMP pick if it fits your plan

Strategy notes

Adjacency: Liberty Square is a natural pass-through between Fantasyland and Frontierland. Pair with Hall of Presidents (no LL, indoor — good for heat or rain breaks) and Pirates of the Caribbean in Adventureland (Multi Pass Tier 2, a few minutes’ walk through the hub).
Can be used as a midday recovery ride — good air conditioning, long queue capacity means waits absorb quickly when manageable.
High repeat value if it appeals to you — not a “one and done” ride for fans.

Trip relevance
High — few Disney rides have Haunted Mansion’s broad universal appeal and iconic status. Worth prioritizing for almost any visitor.
Planning note
Verify status and any seasonal overlays (holiday themed versions typically run only in October/November/December — not expected in April, but verify).

Disney/Parks/Magic-Kingdom/Attractions/P.title: Peter Pan's Flight
Disney/Parks/Magic-Kingdom/Attractions/P.content:
Peter Pan’s Flight

Type

Dark ride (suspended omnimover; “flying” over miniature London and Neverland)

Location

Land: Fantasyland, Magic Kingdom
Central Fantasyland; near Seven Dwarfs Mine Train and It’s a Small World

Community sentiment score

8.8/10 — Sentiment tier: Strong
Basis: public-source consensus reviewed Mar 2026. Planning guidance, not an official rating.

What it is

Classic suspended dark ride where vehicles hang below the track and “fly” over miniature scenes of London and Neverland. Based on Peter Pan. Short ride (~3 min), simple by modern standards, but perpetually high in demand due to nostalgia and the unique flying perspective.

Physical characteristics

Height requirement: None
Intensity: Very low — gentle movement, no speed, no drops, no motion sickness risk
Water: No
Why it matters despite simplicity: The suspended flying perspective is unique. Guests consistently rate the atmosphere highly. It punches above its technical weight.

Lightning Lane

Multi Pass Tier 1 (max 1 advance pre-book)
Peter Pan is almost always in Tier 1 despite its age — standby builds to 50–90 min regularly because the ride capacity is low and demand is high
Key trade-off: Using your Tier 1 slot on Peter Pan means you cannot also pre-book Space Mountain, Tiana’s Bayou Adventure, or Jungle Cruise in the same advance window. Decide which Tier 1 ride matters most.

Strategy notes

Do not do Peter Pan’s Flight on standby during peak hours — the wait-to-experience ratio is poor (3 min ride, 60+ min standby).
Best managed via LLMP Tier 1 pre-book early in the morning, or via early entry standby before the park opens to the general public.
Adjacency: In the heart of Fantasyland with Seven Dwarfs Mine Train, It’s a Small World, Winnie the Pooh, and Dumbo — excellent cluster value if planning a Fantasyland block.

Trip relevance
High — one of the most iconic rides at Walt Disney World. The “flying over London” perspective is uniquely charming and not replicated elsewhere in the park.
Planning note
Verify operating status closer to trip.

Disney/Parks/Magic-Kingdom/Attractions/S.title: Seven Dwarfs Mine Train
Disney/Parks/Magic-Kingdom/Attractions/S.content:
Seven Dwarfs Mine Train

Type

Coaster (family roller coaster; swinging mine cars)

Location

Land: Fantasyland, Magic Kingdom
Center of Fantasyland; near It’s a Small World and Peter Pan’s Flight

Community sentiment score

9.4/10 — Sentiment tier: Elite
Basis: public-source consensus reviewed Mar 2026. Planning guidance, not an official rating.

What it is

Family coaster through gem-mining caverns themed to Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs. Vehicles sway side to side during the ride. Mix of indoor animated scenes and outdoor track sections. Short ride (~3 min) but very popular.

Physical characteristics

Height requirement: 38” (97 cm) — lower bar than most coasters; accessible for most adults and older children
Intensity: Mild-to-moderate — gentle speeds, soft forces, swinging cars add novelty not roughness
Motion sickness risk: Low for most guests
Water: No
Drop: Small dips; nothing significant

Lightning Lane

Single Pass only — not included in Multi Pass
Approx. price: 11–15/person (one of the cheaper Single Passes at WDW)
Less expensive than TRON; still in high demand due to broad family appeal
On moderate-crowd late-April days, standby can sometimes be managed vs. TRON (which almost always warrants Single Pass or rope drop). Compare waits on the day.

Strategy notes

Adjacency advantage: Peter Pan’s Flight (Multi Pass Tier 1) and It’s a Small World (Tier 2) are immediately adjacent in Fantasyland — efficient cluster for Fantasyland coverage in one block.
If saving budget vs. buying both Single Passes (TRON + Seven Dwarfs): prioritize TRON for Single Pass and attempt Seven Dwarfs via LLMP Tier 2 refresh or standby during a lull.
Still builds waits early — don’t assume you can walk up freely by 9:30 AM.

Trip relevance
High — one of the most iconic family coasters at Walt Disney World. Clear priority unless you have no interest in family coasters.
Planning note
Verify operating status closer to trip.
Park: Magic Kingdom

Disney/Parks/Magic-Kingdom/Attractions/S.title: Space Mountain
Disney/Parks/Magic-Kingdom/Attractions/S.content:
Space Mountain

Type

Coaster (indoor dark coaster; space/galaxy theme)

Location

Land: Tomorrowland, Magic Kingdom
The iconic dome structure in the center of Tomorrowland; easy to navigate to from park entrance

Community sentiment score

8.7/10 — Sentiment tier: Strong
Basis: public-source consensus reviewed Mar 2026. Planning guidance, not an official rating.

What it is

Classic indoor roller coaster themed as a space journey. Track is not visible in the dark — the disorientation is part of the experience. Relatively mild by modern coaster standards, but darkness makes it feel more intense than it is. One of Magic Kingdom’s most iconic experiences.
Ride duration: ~3 min

Physical characteristics

Height requirement: 44” (113 cm)
Intensity: Moderate — faster than it appears, rough bumps and abrupt turns, no inversions. The darkness creates a psychological intensity effect.
Motion sickness risk: Moderate — the sudden turns in the dark can disorient sensitive guests more than a visible coaster would
Water: No
Drop: No major drops; the track is not steep

Lightning Lane

Multi Pass Tier 1 (max 1 advance pre-book)
Same Tier 1 pool as Peter Pan’s Flight, Jungle Cruise, and Tiana’s Bayou Adventure — pick one per advance booking
Standby can build to 40–75 min; worth using Tier 1 LLMP if this is a priority vs. Peter Pan

Strategy notes

Best time: Early morning (rope drop), early entry, or via LLMP Tier 1 booking.
Adjacency: Tomorrowland cluster with TRON Lightcycle / Run, Buzz Lightyear, Monsters Inc. Laugh Floor, and PeopleMover. TRON and Space Mountain are the two headliners — don’t try to rope-drop both on standby; LL one of them.
If you have TRON Single Pass booked for a morning window, use early entry to do Space Mountain standby before the park opens.

Trip relevance
High for coaster fans and nostalgia-driven visitors; moderate for guests who prioritize newer or more intense experiences.
Planning note
Verify operating status closer to trip.
Park: Magic Kingdom

Disney/Parks/Magic-Kingdom/Attractions/T.title: TRON Lightcycle  -  Run
Disney/Parks/Magic-Kingdom/Attractions/T.content:
TRON Lightcycle / Run

Type

Coaster (outdoor/indoor launch coaster; motorcycle-position seating)

Location

Land: Tomorrowland, Magic Kingdom
Far end of Tomorrowland; the large blue canopy structure is visible from the park entrance

Community sentiment score

9.6/10 — Sentiment tier: Elite
Basis: public-source consensus reviewed Mar 2026. Planning guidance, not an official rating.

What it is

High-speed launch coaster where guests straddle a lightcycle vehicle and lean forward. Launches through an outdoor canopy with lighting effects, briefly enters an enclosed building, then back outside. Based on the TRON franchise.
One of the fastest rides at Walt Disney World. Ride duration is short (~2.5 min).

Physical characteristics

Height requirement: 48” (122 cm)
Intensity: High — rapid launch acceleration, high speeds, aggressive lateral movement. The motorcycle lean position is unusual and can feel uncomfortable for guests with upper-body sensitivity.
Motion sickness risk: Moderate-high for guests sensitive to speed and lateral forces. No inversions. No spinning.
Water: No
Drop: No significant drops; speed is the primary intensity driver

Lightning Lane

Single Pass only — not included in Multi Pass
Approx. price: 19–23/person (dynamic; verify in MDE app)
Pop Century booking day: 7 days before your MK date. Book at 7 AM.
Alternative: standby is shortest in the first 15–30 min of park opening (or early entry window). Go directly to TRON if using rope drop strategy.

Strategy notes

Best approached as either rope-drop-first or Single Pass. Do not assume midday standby will be manageable.
If using rope drop: walk directly to TRON from the park entrance. Do not stop for photos or food first.
If buying Single Pass: you can use early entry time for another ride (e.g., Space Mountain or Seven Dwarfs) while TRON Single Pass fills your window.
Adjacency: Space Mountain is close. Do not cluster both at morning opening without LL planning — they compete for the same scarce morning window.

Trip relevance
High — MK’s newest headliner and clearest “must-do for coaster fans” priority.
Planning note

Disney/Resorts/Disney's-Pop-Century-Reso.title: Disney's Pop Century Resort
Disney/Resorts/Disney's-Pop-Century-Reso.content:
Disney’s Pop Century Resort

Trip-specific facts

Stay dates: 18 Apr 2026 → 24 Apr 2026
Room: Standard Room
This is your confirmed Disney hotel.

Why it matters operationally
Pop Century is not just a place to sleep. It affects rope-drop reliability and midday-break viability.
Important planning advantages

Eligible for Early Theme Park Entry.
Disney transportation page indicates Pop Century is connected to the Disney Skyliner network for Hollywood Studios and EPCOT access.
Disney also indicates complimentary bus transportation is available across the resort system, including theme parks and Disney Springs.

Best practical uses

Strong base for Hollywood Studios and EPCOT days because Skyliner access can reduce friction.
Good option for a midday reset if one of those two park days becomes too hot or too long.
Less direct strategic advantage for Magic Kingdom than for the Skyliner parks, so that day likely needs an earlier start.

Current trip fit

Confirmed base for the Disney segment: 18 Apr 2026 → 24 Apr 2026.
Best aligned with the current first-pass Disney structure:

18 Apr — transfer day with Magic Kingdom as a candidate park day
20 Apr — EPCOT candidate
21 Apr — Hollywood Studios candidate
23 Apr — Animal Kingdom candidate


19 Apr currently reads well as a rest / pool day candidate.
22 Apr currently reads well as a shopping / flex day candidate.
Operationally, Pop Century is especially strong if EPCOT and Hollywood Studios remain on 20–21 Apr because of Skyliner access.

Watchouts

Transportation queues themselves can become the bottleneck.
Exact route timing is variable and should be checked during the trip.
If driving instead of using Disney transport, compare convenience versus parking / arrival stress park by park.

Which Disney days should rely on Skyliner vs car

Disney/Strategy/April-2026-Context.title: April 2026 Context
Disney/Strategy/April-2026-Context.content:
April 2026 Context
Your Disney window: 18–24 Apr 2026
This is a good timing window. Mid-to-late April is consistently one of the calmer stretches of the spring season at Walt Disney World.

Crowd pattern for April 2026






























DatesCrowd levelNotes30 Mar – 5 AprVery high (worst week)Easter week; peak spring break; most crowded window of spring 20266–11 AprHigh (tapering)Post-Easter; Northeast and Midwest schools still on break12–17 AprModerateYour Universal dates — crowds easing; Epic Universe still in high demand18–24 AprModerate to lowYour Disney dates — good timing
Easter 2026 falls on 5 Apr. You arrive at Disney on 18 Apr, which is 13 days after Easter. The worst crowds will have cleared.

What is open, what is closed — April 2026
Confirmed closed during your stay

























AttractionStatusExpected reopeningBig Thunder Mountain RailroadClosedEarly May 2026 (likely not operational 18–24 Apr; verify)Rock ‘n’ Roller CoasterRetheming to MuppetsSummer 2026 — very unlikely to be open during your Disney stayRivers of America / Tom Sawyer Island / Liberty Square RiverboatPermanently closed Jul 2025Being replaced with Piston Peak / Cars land expansion
Reopening during or just before your stay













AttractionStatusBuzz Lightyear’s Space Ranger SpinReopening 8 Apr 2026 — should be available during your Disney stay
Items to verify closer to trip

Test Track operational status (was under refurbishment in late 2024)
Exact Big Thunder Mountain reopening date (early May 2026 is current estimate; may slip)
Any temporary closures for maintenance in late April


EPCOT International Flower & Garden Festival
Your entire Disney stay (18–24 Apr) falls within the festival window.

Festival dates: 4 Mar – 1 Jun 2026
Included with regular EPCOT admission — no extra cost
20+ outdoor kitchen food booths with small-plate food and drinks (~5–10/item)
Character topiaries throughout World Showcase
Garden Rocks Concert Series on select evenings (some require separate ticket)

See Flower-Garden-Festival-2026 for booth list and strategy.
Planning implication: EPCOT during Flower & Garden is materially different from a normal day — the World Showcase becomes a food stroll, not just country pavilions. Build at least 2–3 hours into the EPCOT day for booth grazing if this appeals to you.

April weather in Orlando

Average high: 83°F (28°C)
Average low: 62°F (17°C)
Rain: April has ~10 rain days on average; brief afternoon thunderstorms are common from mid-afternoon onward
Humidity: Noticeable but not as oppressive as June–August
Practical notes:

Carry a light rain layer or small umbrella
Afternoon downpours are short — wait them out in an indoor attraction
Morning is the most comfortable outdoor window; use it for outdoor queues and safari
April is warm enough that water rides feel good by late morning




Festival and seasonal offerings





























FestivalParkDatesYour overlapEPCOT Flower & GardenEPCOT4 Mar – 1 Jun 2026Full overlap (18–24 Apr)Disney Festival of Fantasy ParadeMagic KingdomDaily (verify schedule)Depends on schedule at visitFantasmic!Hollywood StudiosSelect evenings (verify)Depends on schedule

Impact on planning

Drop Big Thunder Mountain from all MK plan versions — it will not be available.
Drop Rock ‘n’ Roller Coaster from all DHS plan versions — almost certain it’s still closed.
Add EPCOT Flower & Garden as a feature of your EPCOT day, not just a ride day.
Crowd levels in late April mean LLMP day-of refresh should be more available than in peak season — but still book LLSP for TRON, Guardians, Rise, and Flight of Passage early.
Afternoon rain risk: plan indoor-heavy mid-afternoons when possible. Animal Kingdom and Magic Kingdom have the most outdoor queue exposure.

Disney/Strategy/Dining-Reservations.title: Dining Reservations
Disney/Strategy/Dining-Reservations.content:
Disney Dining Reservations
Booking window

60 days in advance of the dining date.
Pop Century resort benefit: You can book dining for your entire stay starting 60 days before your check-in date.

Check-in: 18 Apr → first booking day: 17 Feb 2026 (has already passed as of Mar 21, 2026).


Current situation (as of 21 Mar 2026): The 60-day window for each remaining date is open day-by-day. Today you can book through ~20 May; for Apr 21 onward, you are already within the 60-day window.

Action if no reservations have been made yet: Book as soon as possible. You are not too late for most restaurants, but the hardest-to-get spots will already have limited availability.
Booking logistics

Reservations open at 6:00 AM Eastern.
Fan sites recommend being logged into the My Disney Experience app or website at 5:45 AM — some slots release slightly early.
Have your party size, date, and restaurant target ready before the clock hits.


Hardest reservations to get
These fill up fast and require booking at the first possible minute or repeatedly checking for cancellations.



























































RestaurantParkWhy it’s hardNotesCinderella’s Royal TableMagic KingdomInside the castle; limited coversFull character breakfast, lunch, dinnerBe Our Guest RestaurantMagic KingdomHigh demand for castle-area table serviceDinner is harder than lunchSpace 220 RestaurantEPCOTUnique space-station conceptWalk-up list sometimes availableTopolino’s TerraceDisney’s Riviera ResortCharacter breakfast; rooftop viewsOff-site but popular for Disney Springs/Skyliner accessOga’s CantinaHollywood StudiosBar, no food; limited seatingReservations recommended; walk-up often unavailableThe Hollywood Brown DerbyHollywood StudiosFlagship TS; small roomSci-Fi Dine-In TheaterHollywood StudiosUnique themed experienceNot as hard but still worth bookingTiffinsAnimal KingdomMost upscale AK optionEasiest of the “hard” list
Cancellations: Worth checking repeatedly 2–4 weeks out and even 1–2 days out. Disney diners frequently cancel.

Practical dining by park
Magic Kingdom

Limited table service compared to other parks.
Worth a reservation: Be Our Guest (dinner), Cinderella’s Royal Table (if princess/castle experience matters).
Quick service that works: Columbia Harbour House (Liberty Square; seafood, no characters, indoor seating), Pinocchio Village Haus (Fantasyland, overlooks Small World boat).
Skip: Most Tomorrowland fast food options.

EPCOT

Most dining options in all of Walt Disney World. World Showcase has a full-service restaurant in nearly every pavilion.
Flower & Garden Festival (your dates): Outdoor kitchen booths supplement every restaurant. If you want to food-stroll, you don’t need a table service reservation.
Worth a reservation: Space 220 (for the experience), Les Chefs de France (solid French food), Tokyo Dining or Teppan Edo (Japan), Via Napoli (pizza, Italy), Coral Reef Restaurant (World Nature, dining overlooking the aquarium).
Quick service that works: Festival booth grazing is itself a meal if you plan 4–6 booth stops.

Hollywood Studios

Worth a reservation: Oga’s Cantina (book in advance), Sci-Fi Dine-In Theater (unique; fun even if food is average), The Hollywood Brown Derby (if you want a proper meal).
Avoid: Docking Bay 7 is quick-service but usually has long waits during lunch peak.
If Fantasmic! is in your plan: Consider dining earlier in the day so the evening is free.

Animal Kingdom

Worth a reservation: Tiffins (if one splurge AK meal makes sense), Tusker House (character breakfast if characters matter; otherwise skip for efficiency).
Quick service that works: Satu’li Canteen (Pandora; best QS in the park), Harambe Market (good variety), Flame Tree Barbecue (great outdoor seating).
Practical note: AK is often done by mid-afternoon; you may prefer a lighter in-park meal and a proper dinner back at the resort or Disney Springs.


Disney Springs

No park ticket required.
Has the most upscale and varied restaurant options in the Disney World area.
Worth a reservation if you have a non-park evening:

The BOATHOUSE
Wine Bar George
Paddlefish
Splitsville Luxury Lanes (food is better than expected)


Disney Springs is a good choice for the evening of 18 Apr (check-in day) or any lighter day where you do not stay in-park for dinner.


Cancellation policy

Most restaurants: cancel up to 2 hours before the reservation without a fee.
Some signature restaurants: 24-hour cancellation required to avoid a per-person no-show charge (~10–25/person).
Always check the cancellation policy when booking.


Summary action items



































ActionWhenNotesBook hardest ADRs (Cinderella’s, Be Our Guest, Space 220, Oga’s)ImmediatelyWindow is already open; do it now via MDE appBook EPCOT table service if wantedNowNot as critical with festival booths availableDecide whether Tiffins (AK) or Hollywood Brown Derby (DHS) is worth itBefore tripGood for one mid-trip splurge mealVerify restaurant status and hours~4 weeks beforeSome seasonal closures or schedule changes in late AprilCheck for cancellations on any missed hard reservations2–4 weeks before, again 2–3 days beforeCommon window for drops

Disney/Strategy/Early-Theme-Park-Entry.title: Early Theme Park Entry
Disney/Strategy/Early-Theme-Park-Entry.content:
Early Theme Park Entry
What is confirmed
Disney’s official early-entry page indicates that guests staying at Disney Resort hotels get 30-minute early entry at Walt Disney World theme parks, including check-in and check-out days, with valid admission required. Pop Century is listed as an eligible Disney Resort hotel.
What this means for this trip
Since you are staying at Disney’s Pop Century Resort, you should plan around early entry on Disney days.
Practical strategy

Arrive at the park before the early-entry window starts; do not aim to arrive at the exact opening minute.
Use early entry for the park’s highest-friction ride cluster, not for a low-demand warm-up.
Keep the first 90 minutes disciplined: headliners first, photos/snacks later.
If using Disney transport, budget for queueing at the transport system itself, not just park security.

Park-by-park default use

Magic Kingdom: often best used to knock out one or two major Tomorrowland / Fantasyland priorities early.
EPCOT: best used to get ahead on major rides before World Showcase pace takes over.
Disney’s Hollywood Studios: one of the most valuable parks for early entry because several top attractions stack waits quickly.
Disney’s Animal Kingdom: very strong early-entry value, especially if the goal is Pandora or an early safari.

Variable items to verify closer to trip

Exact early-entry start times
Which attractions are participating that week
Whether any headliner has altered access rules

Operational note
Early entry matters most when paired with a clear first, second, and third attraction plan.

Disney/Strategy/Lightning-Lane.title: Lightning Lane
Disney/Strategy/Lightning-Lane.content:
Disney Lightning Lane Strategy
System overview (2025–2026)
Disney currently sells three skip-the-line products. You do not need to buy all of them.

























ProductWhat it coversCost rangeLightning Lane Multi Pass (LLMP)Most attractions — tiered system, pre-book up to 315–45/person/day (dynamic)Lightning Lane Single Pass (LLSP)Only the 5 highest-demand rides; 1 per purchase11–25/person/ride (dynamic)Lightning Lane Premier PassEverything — all LLMP + all LLSP, unlimitedUp to $449/person/day (peak; generally not worth it except Easter week)
For this trip: LLMP + selective LLSP purchases is the practical approach for most days.

Multi Pass rules

Book up to 3 attractions per day at park opening (or 7 days ahead as Disney resort guests at Pop Century).
At tiered parks (MK, EPCOT, DHS), you can only pre-book 1 Tier 1 attraction — the rest must be Tier 2.
After using a booking (or 2 hours pass), you can add a new one.
Animal Kingdom has no tiers — pick any 3 from the Multi Pass pool.

Single Pass rules

Maximum 2 Single Passes per person per day, across all parks.
Same 7-day/3-day advance window as Multi Pass (resort guests / off-site).
Can be bought without Multi Pass.
Sell out fast on busy days — book at 7 AM (or earlier on peak dates) if you want them.


Single Pass rides — the only 5
These rides are not in Multi Pass. You buy a separate Single Pass per ride.









































ParkRideApprox. price rangeNotesMagic KingdomTRON Lightcycle / Run19–23Usually the busiest MK rideMagic KingdomSeven Dwarfs Mine Train11–15Lower price reflects slower demand than TRONEPCOTGuardians of the Galaxy: Cosmic Rewind16–22Frequently sells outHollywood StudiosStar Wars: Rise of the Resistance20–25Most expensive LLSP at WDWAnimal KingdomAvatar: Flight of Passage15–19Often sells out by 9 AM on busy days
All prices are dynamic and will be higher during Easter week and other peak dates.
Prices sourced from 2025–early 2026 reports; verify in My Disney Experience closer to trip.

Magic Kingdom — tier list

























TierAttractionsTier 1 (max 1 pre-book)Space Mountain, Peter Pan’s Flight, Jungle Cruise, Tiana’s Bayou AdventureTier 2Haunted Mansion, Pirates of the Caribbean, Buzz Lightyear’s Space Ranger Spin, It’s a Small World, Monsters Inc. Laugh Floor, Mickey’s PhilharMagic, Dumbo, Mad Tea Party, The Barnstormer, Tomorrowland Speedway, Under the Sea, Magic Carpets of AladdinSingle Pass onlyTRON Lightcycle / Run, Seven Dwarfs Mine TrainNo LLPeopleMover, Carousel of Progress, Hall of Presidents, Enchanted Tales with Belle, Tiki Room
Note: Big Thunder Mountain Railroad is closed through early May 2026 — it will not appear in any LL pool during your Apr 18–24 Disney stay.
Recommended LLMP pick: Tier 1 = Tiana’s Bayou Adventure or Peter Pan’s Flight. Add Haunted Mansion + Pirates or Buzz as Tier 2.
Recommended LLSP: TRON (highest standby, clearest payoff). Seven Dwarfs is cheaper and lower pressure but useful.

EPCOT — tier list

























TierAttractionsTier 1 (max 1 pre-book)Frozen Ever After, Remy’s Ratatouille Adventure, Test TrackTier 2Soarin’ Around the World, Mission: SPACE, Living with the Land, The Seas with Nemo & Friends, Spaceship Earth, Turtle Talk with Crush, Journey Into ImaginationSingle Pass onlyGuardians of the Galaxy: Cosmic RewindNo LLLuminous, festival booths, World Showcase pavilions
Note: Test Track was undergoing refurbishment in late 2024 — verify status closer to trip.
Recommended LLMP pick: Tier 1 = Remy’s or Frozen. Add Soarin’ + one more Tier 2.
Recommended LLSP: Guardians of the Galaxy: Cosmic Rewind — extremely long standby, LLSP is usually the right call.

Disney’s Hollywood Studios — tier list

























TierAttractionsTier 1 (max 1 pre-book)Slinky Dog Dash, Mickey & Minnie’s Runaway Railway, Millennium Falcon: Smugglers RunTier 2Tower of Terror, Toy Story Mania!, Star Tours, Indiana Jones Epic Stunt Spectacular, Alien Swirling Saucers, Frozen Sing-AlongSingle Pass onlyStar Wars: Rise of the ResistanceNo LLFantasmic!, Galaxy’s Edge atmosphere, most shows
Note: Rock ‘n’ Roller Coaster is undergoing a Muppets retheme and is expected to reopen Summer 2026. It is unlikely to be operating during your Apr 18–24 stay.
Recommended LLMP pick: Tier 1 = Slinky Dog Dash or Millennium Falcon. Add Tower of Terror + Toy Story Mania!
Recommended LLSP: Rise of the Resistance — highest standby in the park, easily 80–120 min without LL.

Disney’s Animal Kingdom — no tiers





















PoolAttractionsMulti Pass (any 3)Expedition Everest, Kilimanjaro Safaris, Na’vi River Journey, Kali River Rapids, Festival of the Lion King, Finding Nemo: The Big Blue… and Beyond!, Feathered Friends in Flight, Zootopia: Better Zoogether!Single Pass onlyAvatar: Flight of PassageNo LLMaharajah Jungle Trek, Gorilla Falls Exploration Trail, Tree of Life
Recommended LLMP pick: Expedition Everest + Kilimanjaro Safaris + Na’vi River Journey.
Recommended LLSP: Flight of Passage — highest standby in the park, often 75–120+ min. Book this as early as possible.

Booking timing for Pop Century guests (resort benefit)

You can book LLMP + LLSP 7 days before each park day.
Set a reminder for 6:45–7:00 AM Eastern on the booking day.
Guardians, Rise of the Resistance, and Flight of Passage LLSP can sell out in minutes on busy days.
Mid-to-late April (your dates) is moderate crowd period — booking day-of is risky but sometimes possible for Tier 2. Don’t risk it for LLSP.

General strategy tips

Never use LLMP for a ride with a short standby. Save slots for high-wait rides.
Once you use a LLMP booking, immediately book your next one.
PeopleMover and Carousel of Progress have no LL — good filler while burning standby waits elsewhere.
Use the My Disney Experience app to monitor standby times in real time and book LLMP refreshes.
On Animal Kingdom day: book Flight of Passage LLSP at 7 AM, then build the morning around early standby for Kilimanjaro Safaris (which opens at park opening).


Items to verify closer to trip

Current LLSP prices (they fluctuate; Easter week is highest)
Test Track operational status (was under refurb in late 2024)
Big Thunder Mountain reopening (expected early May 2026; may or may not affect Apr 24)
Rock ‘n’ Roller Coaster / Muppet* Coaster status (expected Summer 2026 reopening)
Premier Pass pricing if you want all-inclusive skip-the-line without juggling individual purchases

Planning/Temporary-Plans---Universal-Two.content:
Temporary Plans - Universal Two-Day Potter Draft
Status
Temporary proposal for review with Rodrigo and Mariangeles. Not locked.
Context

Trip has 2 park-to-park Universal days across Universal Studios Florida and Islands of Adventure.
Early Park Admission is included.
Goal: use early entry in a different park each day, ride Hogwarts Express in both directions, and avoid a rigid 50/50 split that wastes time.

Planning goal
Build a practical 2-day approach for the two classic Universal parks that:

uses one early entry at Islands of Adventure
uses one early entry at Universal Studios Florida
treats Hogwarts Express as an attraction plus transition, not mandatory transport at peak wait times
leaves day 2 flexible for repeats and cleanup

Proposed plan
Day 1 - Islands of Adventure first, then cross to Studios
Main idea
Start with Early Park Admission at Islands of Adventure, hit the highest-demand attractions there first, enjoy Hogsmeade, then cross by Hogwarts Express to Universal Studios Florida.
Morning priorities

Hagrid’s Magical Creatures Motorbike Adventure
Harry Potter and the Forbidden Journey
Hogsmeade walk-through
Optional if timing and interest allow:

Flight of the Hippogriff
Jurassic World VelociCoaster



Midday transition

Take Hogwarts Express from Hogsmeade to King’s Cross when the posted wait looks reasonable.
Do not force the train at a bad time just to stay on a rigid schedule.

Afternoon at Universal Studios Florida

Diagon Alley
Harry Potter and the Escape from Gringotts
Ollivanders / shops / Butterbeer / photos if desired
Non-Potter optional attractions depending on energy and waits:

Revenge of the Mummy
MEN IN BLACK Alien Attack
Transformers
Hollywood Rip Ride Rockit if operating and of interest



Night

Finish in Studios unless the day flow changes materially.
Note what deserves a repeat on day 2.

Day 2 - Universal Studios first, then cross back to Islands of Adventure
Main idea
Start with Early Park Admission at Universal Studios Florida, finish the high-value Studios items early, then ride Hogwarts Express in the opposite direction and use the rest of the day as a flexible cleanup/repeat block.
Morning priorities

Harry Potter and the Escape from Gringotts if not completed well on day 1 or if worth repeating
Diagon Alley with lower early-day crowds
Remaining major Studios priorities

Midday transition

Take Hogwarts Express from King’s Cross to Hogsmeade.
This direction is often described by fans as especially strong for a first full reveal, but with 2 days available the better move is simply to do both directions without rushing.

Afternoon at Islands of Adventure
Use the rest of the day for any mix of:

Hagrid’s repeat
VelociCoaster
Spider-Man
Hulk
Jurassic Park River Adventure
More time in Hogsmeade
Flexible repeat time in whichever park is winning

Night

Close in the park that feels strongest after day-of conditions.

Hogwarts Express recommendation

Ride it at least once in each direction.
Preferred mindset: attraction plus themed transition.
Avoid using it as mandatory transportation during peak waits if it starts eating too much park time.

Why this proposal works

One early entry in each park
Hogwarts Express covered both ways
Day 1 attacks the most sensitive IOA priorities first
Day 2 acts as recovery space for missed rides, bad waits, or favorites worth repeating
Better than a rigid half-day split because it keeps decisions responsive to actual crowd conditions

Short version for chat

Day 1: start in Islands of Adventure, do Hogsmeade priorities, cross by train to Universal Studios.
Day 2: start in Universal Studios, do Diagon Alley priorities, cross by train back to Islands of Adventure.
Keep the second day flexible.

Notes from quick external research

Universal official materials indicate Hogwarts Express requires park-to-park admission and the experience differs by direction.
Fan discussions repeatedly suggest riding both directions and avoiding peak-hour train waits when trying to maximize rides.
A common fan preference is to experience King’s Cross to Hogsmeade as a strong first-time reveal, but this is a preference note, not a hard rule.

Open questions before locking

Are Rodrigo and Mariangeles prioritizing Harry Potter atmosphere or maximizing headliner ride count?
Is VelociCoaster a must-do, optional, or skip?
Are they likely to want a slower lunch / shopping block in Diagon Alley or Hogsmeade?
Should this remain a flexible framework or be converted into a timed step-by-step itinerary?
